changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforcement to k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforced for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to transmission confidentiality and integrity through TLS enforcement. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a minor change was made to align with the pattern of using past participle for action descriptions.
naming_improvements: Use 'enforced' instead of 'enforcement' to maintain consistency with naming conventions
title: Transmission Confidentiality And Integrity - Transmission confidentiality and integrity | cryptographic protection for messag
step1_reasoning: The control SC-8(3) focuses on ensuring transmission confidentiality and integrity through cryptographic protection. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring TLS is enforced for network communications, both at the ingress level and within the cluster. NetworkPolicies can be used to enforce TLS for pod-to-pod communication, while Ingress configurations should ensure TLS is enabled for external communications. Additionally, etcd and the API server should have TLS enabled to protect data in transit.
